task_completion.description: Import the Hurricane Katrina track data from the CSV file into GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra Spreadsheet view is open and populated with the CSV data (Longitude and Latitude columns), or the Algebra view shows a list of points created from the imported data.

task_completion.description: Visualize the storm's path by mapping Longitude to X and Latitude to Y.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Points are visible in the GeoGebra Graphics view. The coordinates should reflect the data (e.g., X values around -75 to -90, Y values around 23 to 35).

task_completion.description: Create a polyline connecting the track points in chronological order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A continuous line or a series of connected line segments (polyline) links the plotted points sequentially in the GeoGebra Graphics view.

task_completion.description: Calculate the total distance and display it on the screen using a formatted text label.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text object is visible in the GeoGebra Graphics view with the exact format 'Total Distance: [value] km'. The value should be approximately 2401.6 (acceptable range is 2000 to 3000).

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'katrina_analysis.ggb' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra application window title bar displays 'katrina_analysis.ggb', or a file explorer/terminal window shows the file 'katrina_analysis.ggb' successfully saved in the ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GeoGebra's menus, spreadsheet, algebra input, and graphics views rather than using a Python script or terminal commands to generate the output file.

integrity.description: The distance label was created within GeoGebra as part of the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text label 'Total Distance: [value] km' is rendered inside the GeoGebra Graphics view, not just typed into a separate text editor or overlaid using an external image editing tool.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved a valid GeoGebra project file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog in GeoGebra was used, or the file appears with the correct .ggb extension in the file manager, rather than the agent using terminal commands like 'touch katrina_analysis.ggb' to create an empty dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The actual cumulative track distance is approximately 2401.6 km (acceptable range: 2000 to 3000 km). The conversion factor is 111.32 km per degree. The first data point plotted should be at X=-75.1, Y=23.1 and the second at X=-76.5, Y=24.5. The final saved file must be exactly named 'katrina_analysis.ggb'.
